blog.title: The follow-up nobody sends
blog.dek: Most service businesses lose deals in the silence after the quote.
blog.markdown:
# The follow-up nobody sends

*Most service businesses lose deals in the silence after the quote.*

You didn’t lose the job. You lost the follow-up. It shows up the same way across almost every shop we look at — quietly, in places nobody's watching.

## The silence after the quote.

A quote goes out. Then nothing. No nudge, no reminder, no second touch.

The customer didn’t say no. They went quiet — and you let them.

> **80%** of sales need five follow-ups. Most businesses stop after one.

## Let an operator do the chasing.

Five touches, spaced and personal — sent without you lifting a finger.

It never forgets, never gets busy, never lets a quote go cold.

---

**Where are your follow-ups leaking?** We’ll map the touches you’re missing — and the operator that sends them for you. [Free follow-up audit · elenos.ai](https://elenos.ai)

blog.title: The job goes to whoever calls back first
blog.dek: Speed-to-lead beats price — and an AI operator wins it while you are on the job.
blog.markdown:
# The job goes to whoever calls back first

*Speed-to-lead beats price — and an AI operator wins it while you are on the job.*

The job didn’t go to the best contractor. It went to the one who called back first. It shows up the same way across almost every shop we look at — quietly, in places nobody's watching.

## Every missed call is a quote you’ll never send.

A lead calls at 2pm. You’re under a sink. You call back at six. By six, they’ve booked someone else.

It never felt like a lost job. It felt like a voicemail.

> **100×** more likely to reach a lead when you call back in five minutes — not thirty.

## An operator that answers in nine seconds.

While you’re on the ladder, it picks up, qualifies the job, and books the slot.

Not a chatbot. An operator — it retires the task, not the human.

---

**Where do your leads leak?** We’ll trace every call, form, and follow-up — and show you what’s quietly costing you booked work. [Free 20-minute teardown · elenos.ai](https://elenos.ai)

blog.title: Three things on every contractor site that cost jobs
blog.dek: A diagnostic teardown of the website mistakes that quietly cost contractors booked work.
blog.markdown:
# Three things on every contractor site that cost jobs

*A diagnostic teardown of the website mistakes that quietly cost contractors booked work.*

Three things we see on almost every contractor site — and each one costs booked work. It shows up the same way across almost every shop we look at — quietly, in places nobody's watching.

## Make the number a button.

No click-to-call on mobile. 60–70% of your visitors are on a phone.

Every extra tap is friction. Friction kills momentum. And lost momentum is lost revenue.

The number should be one tap. Front and center. Right where the decision happens.

## Write the hero for them — not you.

“Family owned since 1998” is nice. But it’s about you.

“24/7 emergency AC repair in Gloucester County — here tonight.” That’s what gets the call.

## Put the proof where the decision happens.

200 five-star reviews — and zero of them on your homepage.

They live on Google. Move them to where people decide.

---

**Want me to look at yours?** We’ll show you exactly where visitors are dropping off — and what to fix first. [Free 20-minute teardown · elenos.ai](https://elenos.ai)
